reasoning:
The fixed baseline of 11.083 requires strictly >=12 protests in a 30-day period. Assuming a Poisson distribution with mean 11.083, the base rate probability of success is roughly 44%. However, on March 13-14, 2025, the Prime Minister explicitly threatened to lead widespread street protests if the five embattled members of the Judicial Council do not resign voluntarily (following a parliamentary vote of no-confidence). In North Macedonia, ruling-party orchestrated protests typically occur nationwide across multiple locations, which ACLED codes as distinct events, meaning the threshold of 12 can be easily surpassed in a short time. 

For the first window (resolving Mar 23), the 30 days cover late Feb to late Mar. Given we are already late into this period, some background protests have likely accumulated, and imminent PM-led protests could push this over the edge. For the second window (Apr 15), the entire 30 days falls squarely after the PM's threat, creating the highest probability of surpassing the threshold. For more distant dates, the immediate political crisis is likely to be resolved, so probabilities naturally regress toward the base rate (44%), with slightly elevated chances for residual or seasonal unrest.
